# MCP-UI, Gradio, and Evidence.dev Integration Analysis

**Research Date**: 2025-11-22
**Focus**: Deep analysis of how MCP-UI can serve as an integration layer for artifacts generated by Gradio and Evidence.dev

---

## Executive Summary

This research explores how **MCP-UI** (Model Context Protocol User Interface) can serve as an orchestration and delivery layer for interactive artifacts generated by **Gradio** (ML demo framework) and **Evidence.dev** (SQL-based BI framework).

**Key Finding**: Yes, MCP-UI can effectively serve as an integration layer for both Gradio and Evidence.dev artifacts through multiple integration patterns, with Gradio offering the most seamless integration due to native MCP support.

---

1. [Technology Overview](#technology-overview)
2. [Architecture Analysis](#architecture-analysis)

## Technology Overview

### MCP-UI

**What it is**: An SDK that extends the Model Context Protocol to deliver interactive UI components from MCP servers to client applications.

**Core Capabilities**:
- Delivers rich, dynamic UI resources via MCP
- Three content delivery methods:
  1. **Inline HTML** (text/html) - srcDoc in sandboxed iframe
  2. **Remote URLs** (text/uri-list) - External resources loaded in iframe
  3. **Remote DOM** (application/vnd.mcp-ui.remote-dom) - JavaScript-based lightweight rendering
- Sandboxed execution for security
- Bidirectional communication between embedded UI and host
- Multi-language SDKs: TypeScript, Python, Ruby

**Key Features**:
- `@mcp-ui/server` - Generate UI resources on MCP server
- `@mcp-ui/client` - Render UI resources (React + Web Components)
- `UIResourceRenderer` - Main rendering component
- Security through iframe sandboxing

**Sources**:
- [MCP-UI GitHub](https://github.com/idosal/mcp-ui)
- [MCP-UI Documentation](https://mcpui.dev/guide/introduction)
- [Shopify Engineering: MCP UI](https://shopify.engineering/mcp-ui-breaking-the-text-wall)

---

### Gradio

**What it is**: A Python library for building ML demos and web applications without HTML/CSS/JavaScript.

**Core Capabilities**:
- 30+ interactive components (text, images, audio, video, dataframes, plots)
- Three APIs: Interface (simple), Blocks (flexible), ChatInterface (chatbots)
- Event-driven architecture
- Streaming support with generators
- Progress tracking with decorators
- Built-in authentication
- Theming system with 8 themes
- Custom component creation

**Output Formats**:
- **Interactive Web Apps** - Full-featured web applications
- **Shareable URLs** - Temporary public URLs via `share=True`
- **API Endpoints** - RESTful API access via Gradio Client
- **Embedded Components** - Can be mounted in FastAPI/other frameworks
- **Standalone HTML** - Exported static interfaces

**MCP Integration** (Official Support):
- Launch as MCP server with `mcp_server=True`
- Supports MCP primitives: **tools** (default), **resources**, **prompts**
- Can act as both MCP server AND client
- Official guides: [Building MCP Server with Gradio](https://www.gradio.app/guides/building-mcp-server-with-gradio)

**Sources**:
- [Gradio MCP Server Guide](https://www.gradio.app/guides/building-mcp-server-with-gradio)
- [Gradio MCP Client Guide](https://www.gradio.app/guides/building-an-mcp-client-with-gradio)
- [GitHub Issue #10949](https://github.com/gradio-app/gradio/issues/10949)
- [HuggingFace Blog: Gradio MCP](https://huggingface.co/blog/gradio-mcp)

---

### Evidence.dev

**What it is**: An open-source framework for building data products with SQL and Markdown.

**Core Capabilities**:
- SQL-driven data visualization with DuckDB
- Markdown-first authoring
- 30+ specialized components for data visualization
- Browser-based query execution
- Templated pages with parameters
- Interactive inputs (dropdowns, sliders, date pickers)
- Chart library (ECharts), Maps (Leaflet), UI (Shadcn)

**Output Formats**:
- **Static HTML Sites** - Pre-built HTML (default mode)
- **Single Page Apps (SPA)** - Client-side rendering
- **Embeddable Components** - Via `<Embed>` component
- **Exported Builds** - `npm run build` generates static files

**Embed Support**:
```markdown
<Embed
  url="https://example.com/dashboard"
  title="Dashboard"
  width=800
  height=450
/>
```

**Rendering Modes**:
- **Static** (default): Pre-builds all pages as HTML, no database queries at runtime
- **SPA**: Client-side rendering, suitable for 1,000+ pages

**Sources**:
- [Evidence.dev Embed Component](https://docs.evidence.dev/components/ui/embed)
- [Evidence.dev Rendering Modes](https://docs.evidence.dev/deployment/configuration/rendering-modes)
- [Evidence.dev Documentation](https://docs.evidence.dev/)

---

## Architecture Analysis

### MCP-UI Resource Types and MIME Types

MCP-UI supports three content delivery mechanisms:

| Content Type | MIME Type | Delivery Method | Use Case |
|-------------|-----------|-----------------|----------|
| **Inline HTML** | `text/html` | `srcDoc` in iframe | Self-contained components |
| **Remote URL** | `text/uri-list` | `src` in iframe | External hosted resources |
| **Remote DOM** | `application/vnd.mcp-ui.remote-dom` | JavaScript in Worker | Lightweight, host-rendered UI |

**Security Model**:
- All content executes in sandboxed iframes
- Remote DOM scripts run in Web Workers inside iframes
- UI changes communicated via JSON
- Host controls all rendering and interactions

---

### Gradio Artifact Generation

**Gradio MCP Server Architecture**:

```python
import gradio as gr

def predict_image(image):
    # ML model inference
    return classification_result

demo = gr.Interface(
    fn=predict_image,
    inputs=gr.Image(type="pil"),
    outputs=gr.Label(num_top_classes=5),
    mcp_server=True  # ← Enables MCP server mode
)

demo.launch()
```

**MCP Primitives Exposed**:

1. **Tools** (default) - Execute functions
   - Every Gradio function becomes an MCP tool
   - Input/output mapping automatic

2. **Resources** - Expose data
   ```python
   @gr.mcp_resource("model_info")
   def get_model_info():
       return {"model": "ResNet50", "accuracy": 0.95}
   ```

3. **Prompts** - Reusable templates
   ```python
   @gr.mcp_prompt("classify_prompt")
   def classification_prompt():
       return "Classify this image and return top 5 predictions"
   ```

**Artifact Types Generated**:
- **Web UI**: Full interactive Gradio interface
- **API Endpoints**: RESTful access to functions
- **MCP Tools**: Function calls via MCP protocol
- **Streaming Output**: Generator-based progressive updates

---

### Evidence.dev Artifact Generation

**Evidence Build Process**:

```bash
# Development
npm run dev              # Live preview at http://localhost:3000

# Production
npm run build            # Generates static HTML in /build
```

**Generated Artifacts**:

1. **Static HTML Pages**
   - Pre-rendered pages with embedded DuckDB queries
   - Client-side JavaScript for interactivity
   - Parquet data files for query results

2. **Interactive Components**
   - Charts (ECharts-based SVG/Canvas)
   - Data tables with sorting/filtering
   - Input controls (dropdowns, sliders)
   - Maps (Leaflet-based)

3. **Embeddable Elements**
   - Individual chart exports
   - Dashboard sections
   - Full page embeds

**File Structure of Build Output**:
```
build/
├── index.html              # Entry point
├── dashboard.html          # Individual pages
├── _app/                   # App assets
│   ├── immutable/          # Hashed static files
│   └── version.json
├── data/                   # Parquet files
└── assets/                 # Images, CSS
```

---

## Integration Patterns

### Pattern 1: Gradio MCP Server → MCP-UI Client

**Architecture**:
```
┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐
│  Gradio App     │         │   MCP Protocol  │         │   MCP-UI Host   │
│  (MCP Server)   │────────▶│   (JSON-RPC)    │────────▶│   (Renderer)    │
└─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘
   mcp_server=True              Tool calls                UIResourceRenderer
```

**Implementation**:

**Server (Gradio)**:
```python
import gradio as gr
from mcp_ui_server import UIResource, html_resource

def generate_chart(data_type):
    # Generate interactive chart
    import plotly.express as px
    fig = px.bar(data, x='category', y='value')

    # Return as both Gradio output AND MCP-UI resource
    html_output = fig.to_html(include_plotlyjs='cdn')

    return html_output

demo = gr.Interface(
    fn=generate_chart,
    inputs=gr.Dropdown(["sales", "revenue", "profit"]),
    outputs=gr.HTML(),
    mcp_server=True
)

# MCP-UI resource decorator
@gr.mcp_resource("chart_viewer")
def chart_ui_resource():
    return html_resource(
        html="<div id='chart-container'></div>",
        title="Data Chart Viewer"
    )

demo.launch()
```

**Client (MCP-UI)**:
```typescript
import { UIResourceRenderer } from '@mcp-ui/client';

function GradioChartViewer() {
  return (
    <UIResourceRenderer
      resourceUri="gradio://chart_viewer"
      onEvent={(event) => {
        console.log('Chart interaction:', event);
      }}
    />
  );
}
```

**Benefits**:
- ✅ Native MCP support in Gradio
- ✅ Bidirectional communication
- ✅ Gradio handles all ML/data processing
- ✅ MCP-UI handles UI orchestration
- ✅ Security through MCP protocol

**Challenges**:
- ⚠️ Requires Gradio app to be running (not static)
- ⚠️ Network latency for tool calls

---

### Pattern 2: Evidence Static Export → MCP-UI Remote URL

**Architecture**:
```
┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐
│  Evidence App   │ build   │  Static Files   │ serve   │   MCP-UI Host   │
│  (Dev)          │────────▶│  (HTML/JS/CSS)  │────────▶│   (iframe)      │
└─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘
   npm run build                  /build                  text/uri-list
```

**Implementation**:

**Server (Python MCP Server)**:
```python
from mcp_ui_server import UIResource, url_resource
from mcp.server import Server
import mcp.types as types

app = Server("evidence-dashboard")

@app.list_resources()
async def list_resources() -> list[types.Resource]:
    return [
        types.Resource(
            uri="evidence://sales-dashboard",
            name="Sales Dashboard",
            description="Interactive sales analytics dashboard",
            mimeType="text/uri-list"
        )
    ]

@app.read_resource()
async def read_resource(uri: str) -> str:
    if uri == "evidence://sales-dashboard":
        return url_resource(
            url="https://my-evidence-app.netlify.app/dashboard",
            title="Sales Dashboard"
        )
```

**Client (MCP-UI)**:
```typescript
import { UIResourceRenderer } from '@mcp-ui/client';

function EvidenceDashboard() {
  return (
    <UIResourceRenderer
      resourceUri="evidence://sales-dashboard"
      width="100%"
      height="800px"
    />
  );
}
```

**Benefits**:
- ✅ Static hosting (fast, cheap, scalable)
- ✅ No runtime dependencies
- ✅ Evidence's full component library
- ✅ Works offline after initial load

**Challenges**:
- ⚠️ One-way communication (no events back to MCP)
- ⚠️ Requires separate hosting for Evidence app
- ⚠️ Updates require rebuild and redeploy

---

### Pattern 3: Evidence Embed Component → MCP-UI Inline HTML

**Architecture**:
```
┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────┐
│  Evidence Build │ extract │  HTML Snippet   │ embed   │   MCP-UI Host   │
│  (Static)       │────────▶│  (srcDoc)       │────────▶│   (iframe)      │
└─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘         └─────────────────┘
                                Self-contained              text/html
```

**Implementation**:

**Server (Python MCP Server)**:
```python
from mcp_ui_server import html_resource
import subprocess

def extract_evidence_chart():
    # Build Evidence project
    subprocess.run(["npm", "run", "build"], cwd="/evidence-project")

    # Extract specific chart HTML
    with open("/evidence-project/build/partials/chart.html") as f:
        chart_html = f.read()

    # Wrap with necessary scripts
    complete_html = f"""
    <!DOCTYPE html>
    <html>
    <head>
        <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.evidence.dev/styles.css">
        <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/echarts@5/dist/echarts.min.js"></script>
    </head>
    <body>
        {chart_html}
        <script src="https://cdn.evidence.dev/evidence.js"></script>
    </body>
    </html>
    """

    return html_resource(
        html=complete_html,
        title="Sales Trend Chart"
    )

@app.read_resource()
async def read_resource(uri: str):
    if uri == "evidence://sales-chart":
        return extract_evidence_chart()
```

**Benefits**:
- ✅ Self-contained (no external dependencies)
- ✅ Fast loading
- ✅ Works offline

**Challenges**:
- ⚠️ Manual HTML extraction
- ⚠️ May need to bundle dependencies
- ⚠️ Interactive features may break without proper context

---

### Pattern 4: Unified MCP Server for Both

**Architecture**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         Unified MCP Server                  │
│  ┌──────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐ │
│  │   Gradio     │      │   Evidence      │ │
│  │   Apps       │      │   Dashboards    │ │
│  │              │      │                 │ │
│  │ • ML Models  │      │ • BI Reports    │ │
│  │ • Inference  │      │ • SQL Analytics │ │
│  │ • Real-time  │      │ • Static Export │ │
│  └──────────────┘      └─────────────────┘ │
│           │                     │           │
│           └─────────┬───────────┘           │
│                     │                       │
│         ┌───────────▼──────────┐            │
│         │   MCP-UI Resources   │            │
│         │   • Tools            │            │
│         │   • Resources        │            │
│         │   • Prompts          │            │
│         └──────────────────────┘            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
          ┌─────────────────────┐
          │   MCP-UI Client     │
          │   (Host App)        │
          └─────────────────────┘
```

**Implementation**:

```python
from mcp.server import Server
from mcp_ui_server import html_resource, url_resource
import gradio as gr
import asyncio

app = Server("unified-analytics")

# Gradio ML Interface
def classify_image(image):
    # ML inference
    return predictions

gradio_demo = gr.Interface(
    fn=classify_image,
    inputs=gr.Image(),
    outputs=gr.Label()
)

# Evidence Dashboard URL
EVIDENCE_DASHBOARD_URL = "https://analytics.example.com"

@app.list_tools()
async def list_tools():
    return [
        types.Tool(
            name="classify_image",
            description="Classify an image using ML model",
            inputSchema={
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "image_url": {"type": "string"}
                }
            }
        )
    ]

@app.call_tool()
async def call_tool(name: str, arguments: dict):
    if name == "classify_image":
        # Download image and run Gradio function
        result = gradio_demo.predict(arguments["image_url"])
        return [types.TextContent(type="text", text=str(result))]

@app.list_resources()
async def list_resources():
    return [
        types.Resource(
            uri="app://ml-interface",
            name="ML Classification Interface",
            mimeType="text/uri-list"
        ),
        types.Resource(
            uri="app://analytics-dashboard",
            name="Analytics Dashboard",
            mimeType="text/uri-list"
        )
    ]

@app.read_resource()
async def read_resource(uri: str):
    if uri == "app://ml-interface":
        # Return Gradio interface as URL resource
        gradio_url = gradio_demo.share()  # Get public URL
        return url_resource(url=gradio_url, title="ML Interface")

    elif uri == "app://analytics-dashboard":
        # Return Evidence dashboard
        return url_resource(url=EVIDENCE_DASHBOARD_URL, title="Analytics")
```

**Benefits**:
- ✅ Single MCP server for all capabilities
- ✅ Unified authentication/authorization
- ✅ Coordinated data flow between ML and analytics
- ✅ Simplified client integration

**Challenges**:
- ⚠️ More complex server architecture
- ⚠️ Need to manage multiple service lifecycles
- ⚠️ Potential single point of failure

---

## Technical Implementation

### Example: Complete Integration Stack

**1. Gradio MCP Server (ML Service)**

```python
# ml_service.py
import gradio as gr
from transformers import pipeline
from mcp_ui_server import html_resource

# Load ML model
classifier = pipeline("image-classification")

def classify_and_visualize(image):
    # Run classification
    predictions = classifier(image)

    # Generate visualization
    import plotly.graph_objects as go

    labels = [p['label'] for p in predictions[:5]]
    scores = [p['score'] for p in predictions[:5]]

    fig = go.Figure(data=[
        go.Bar(x=labels, y=scores)
    ])

    fig.update_layout(
        title="Classification Results",
        xaxis_title="Class",
        yaxis_title="Confidence"
    )

    return fig

# Gradio interface with MCP
demo = gr.Interface(
    fn=classify_and_visualize,
    inputs=gr.Image(type="pil", label="Upload Image"),
    outputs=gr.Plot(label="Predictions"),
    title="Image Classification",
    mcp_server=True
)

# MCP Resource for UI
@demo.mcp_resource("classifier_ui")
def classifier_ui():
    return html_resource(
        html="""
        <div class="classifier-container">
            <h2>Image Classifier</h2>
            <input type="file" id="image-upload" accept="image/*">
            <div id="results"></div>
        </div>
        <script>
            // Handle image upload and display results
            document.getElementById('image-upload').addEventListener('change',
                async (e) => {
                    // Call Gradio MCP tool
                    const result = await mcpClient.callTool('classify_image', {
                        image: e.target.files[0]
                    });
                    document.getElementById('results').innerHTML = result;
                }
            );
        </script>
        """,
        title="Image Classifier Interface"
    )

if __name__ == "__main__":
    demo.launch()
```

**2. Evidence Dashboard (Analytics Service)**

```markdown
<!-- evidence-app/pages/ml-analytics.md -->
---
title: ML Model Analytics
---

# ML Model Performance Dashboard

```sql model_predictions
SELECT
    prediction_date,
    model_name,
    COUNT(*) as total_predictions,
    AVG(confidence_score) as avg_confidence,
    SUM(CASE WHEN correct = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as accuracy
FROM predictions
WHERE prediction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1 DESC
```

<Grid cols=3>
    <BigValue
        data={model_predictions}
        value=total_predictions
        title="Total Predictions"
    />
    <BigValue
        data={model_predictions}
        value=avg_confidence
        fmt=pct1
        title="Avg Confidence"
    />
    <BigValue
        data={model_predictions}
        value=accuracy
        fmt=pct1
        title="Accuracy"
    />
</Grid>

## Performance Over Time

<LineChart
    data={model_predictions}
    x=prediction_date
    y=accuracy
    series=model_name
    title="Model Accuracy Trend"
/>

## Prediction Volume

```sql predictions_by_class
SELECT
    predicted_class,
    COUNT(*) as count
FROM predictions
WHERE prediction_date >= DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC
LIMIT 10
```

<BarChart
    data={predictions_by_class}
    x=predicted_class
    y=count
    title="Top Predicted Classes"
    swapXY=true
/>
```

**Build Evidence App**:
```bash
cd evidence-app
npm run build
# Deploy to static hosting (Netlify, Vercel, etc.)
```

**3. MCP Server Orchestrator**

```python
# orchestrator.py
from mcp.server import Server
from mcp_ui_server import url_resource, html_resource
import mcp.types as types
import httpx

app = Server("ml-analytics-platform")

# Service endpoints
GRADIO_URL = "http://localhost:7860"
EVIDENCE_URL = "https://ml-analytics.netlify.app"

@app.list_resources()
async def list_resources():
    return [
        types.Resource(
            uri="platform://ml-interface",
            name="ML Classification Interface",
            description="Interactive image classification with Gradio",
            mimeType="text/uri-list"
        ),
        types.Resource(
            uri="platform://analytics-dashboard",
            name="ML Analytics Dashboard",
            description="Performance metrics and analytics with Evidence",
            mimeType="text/uri-list"
        ),
        types.Resource(
            uri="platform://combined-view",
            name="Combined ML Platform",
            description="Unified interface with ML and analytics",
            mimeType="text/html"
        )
    ]

@app.read_resource()
async def read_resource(uri: str):
    if uri == "platform://ml-interface":
        return url_resource(
            url=GRADIO_URL,
            title="ML Classification"
        )

    elif uri == "platform://analytics-dashboard":
        return url_resource(
            url=EVIDENCE_URL,
            title="Analytics Dashboard"
        )

    elif uri == "platform://combined-view":
        # Return combined HTML view
        combined_html = f"""
        <!DOCTYPE html>
        <html>
        <head>
            <title>ML Analytics Platform</title>
            <style>
                body {{ margin: 0; padding: 0; font-family: sans-serif; }}
                .container {{ display: grid; grid-template-rows: 60px 1fr; height: 100vh; }}
                .header {{ background: #1a1a1a; color: white; padding: 1rem; }}
                .content {{ display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1rem; padding: 1rem; }}
                iframe {{ width: 100%; height: 100%; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; }}
                h1 {{ margin: 0; font-size: 1.5rem; }}
            </style>
        </head>
        <body>
            <div class="container">
                <div class="header">
                    <h1>🤖 ML Analytics Platform</h1>
                </div>
                <div class="content">
                    <div>
                        <h2>Classification Interface</h2>
                        <iframe src="{GRADIO_URL}" title="Gradio ML Interface"></iframe>
                    </div>
                    <div>
                        <h2>Performance Analytics</h2>
                        <iframe src="{EVIDENCE_URL}" title="Evidence Dashboard"></iframe>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
        </body>
        </html>
        """

        return html_resource(
            html=combined_html,
            title="ML Analytics Platform"
        )

@app.list_tools()
async def list_tools():
    return [
        types.Tool(
            name="classify_image",
            description="Classify an image using the ML model",
            inputSchema={
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "image_url": {
                        "type": "string",
                        "description": "URL of image to classify"
                    }
                },
                "required": ["image_url"]
            }
        ),
        types.Tool(
            name="get_model_stats",
            description="Get current model performance statistics",
            inputSchema={
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "model_name": {
                        "type": "string",
                        "description": "Name of the model"
                    }
                }
            }
        )
    ]

@app.call_tool()
async def call_tool(name: str, arguments: dict):
    if name == "classify_image":
        # Call Gradio API
        async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
            response = await client.post(
                f"{GRADIO_URL}/api/predict",
                json={"data": [arguments["image_url"]]}
            )
            result = response.json()

        return [types.TextContent(
            type="text",
            text=f"Classification: {result['data']}"
        )]

    elif name == "get_model_stats":
        # Could query Evidence data source or analytics API
        return [types.TextContent(
            type="text",
            text="Model stats retrieved from analytics dashboard"
        )]
```

**4. MCP-UI Client (React App)**

```typescript
// App.tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { UIResourceRenderer } from '@mcp-ui/client';
import { Client } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js';

function MLAnalyticsPlatform() {
  const [view, setView] = useState('combined');

  const resourceUris = {
    ml: 'platform://ml-interface',
    analytics: 'platform://analytics-dashboard',
    combined: 'platform://combined-view'
  };

  return (
    <div className="platform">
      <header>
        <h1>ML Analytics Platform</h1>
        <nav>
          <button onClick={() => setView('ml')}>
            ML Interface
          </button>
          <button onClick={() => setView('analytics')}>
            Analytics
          </button>
          <button onClick={() => setView('combined')}>
            Combined View
          </button>
        </nav>
      </header>

      <main>
        <UIResourceRenderer
          resourceUri={resourceUris[view]}
          width="100%"
          height="calc(100vh - 80px)"
          onEvent={(event) => {
            console.log('UI Event:', event);
            // Handle events from embedded UIs
          }}
        />
      </main>
    </div>
  );
}

export default MLAnalyticsPlatform;
```

---

## Use Cases

### Use Case 1: AI-Powered Business Intelligence Platform

**Scenario**: A company wants to combine ML predictions with business analytics.

**Components**:
- **Gradio**: Customer churn prediction model
- **Evidence**: Customer analytics dashboard
- **MCP-UI**: Unified interface

**User Flow**:
1. User uploads customer data to Gradio interface
2. Gradio runs churn prediction model
3. Results are stored in database
4. Evidence dashboard auto-updates with new predictions
5. User views combined ML + analytics in MCP-UI

**Architecture**:
```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│          MCP-UI Host Application              │
│                                               │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐    │
│  │  Gradio ML      │  │  Evidence BI    │    │
│  │  Predictions    │  │  Dashboard      │    │
│  │                 │  │                 │    │
│  │ ↓ Churn Score   │  │ → Visualize     │    │
│  │ ↓ Risk Factors  │  │ → Trends        │    │
│  │ ↓ Recommend.    │  │ → KPIs          │    │
│  └────────┬────────┘  └─────────────────┘    │
│           │                    ▲              │
│           │                    │              │
│           └────────►[DB]───────┘              │
│                  Shared Data                  │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

### Use Case 2: E-Commerce Product Recommendations

**Scenario**: Show personalized product recommendations with analytics.

**Components**:
- **Gradio**: Recommendation engine (collaborative filtering)
- **Evidence**: Sales analytics and A/B test results
- **MCP-UI**: Product display interface

**Integration**:
```python
# Gradio recommendation engine
def get_recommendations(user_id, context):
    recommendations = model.predict(user_id, context)

    # Return both recommendations AND analytics link
    return {
        "products": recommendations,
        "analytics_url": f"evidence://user-analytics?user={user_id}"
    }
```

---

### Use Case 3: Data Science Notebook Alternative

**Scenario**: Replace Jupyter notebooks with interactive dashboards.

**Components**:
- **Gradio**: Interactive parameter tuning, model testing
- **Evidence**: Results visualization, experiment tracking
- **MCP-UI**: Notebook-like interface

**Benefits**:
- More interactive than static notebooks
- Real-time collaboration
- Better for non-technical stakeholders
- Version control friendly (Evidence is markdown)

---

### Use Case 4: Multi-Model Ensemble Platform

**Scenario**: Run multiple ML models and compare results.

**Components**:
- **Multiple Gradio Instances**: Each model has its own Gradio app
- **Evidence**: Aggregate results, compare performance
- **MCP-UI**: Orchestrate all models

**Architecture**:
```
MCP-UI Orchestrator
├── Gradio Model A (Image Classification)
├── Gradio Model B (Object Detection)
├── Gradio Model C (Image Segmentation)
└── Evidence Dashboard (Model Comparison)
```

---

## Challenges and Solutions

### Challenge 1: State Synchronization

**Problem**: Gradio and Evidence operate independently; no shared state.

**Solutions**:

1. **Shared Database**:
   ```python
   # Gradio writes predictions
   def predict_and_store(image):
       result = model(image)
       db.insert("predictions", result)
       return result

   # Evidence reads from same DB
   ```

2. **MCP Events**:
   ```python
   # Gradio emits events
   @demo.mcp_event("prediction_complete")
   def on_prediction(result):
       # Evidence can subscribe to this event
       pass
   ```

3. **Message Queue** (Redis/RabbitMQ):
   ```python
   # Gradio publishes
   redis.publish("predictions", json.dumps(result))

   # Evidence subscribes (via source connector)
   ```

---

### Challenge 2: Authentication & Authorization

**Problem**: Different auth systems for Gradio, Evidence, and MCP.

**Solutions**:

1. **Unified Auth in MCP Server**:
   ```python
   @app.before_request
   async def authenticate(request):
       token = request.headers.get("Authorization")
       user = verify_jwt(token)
       request.state.user = user
   ```

2. **OAuth2 Proxy**:
   - Place OAuth2 proxy in front of all services
   - MCP server validates proxy headers

3. **SSO Integration**:
   - Use Auth0, Okta, or similar
   - Single sign-on across all components

---

### Challenge 3: Performance & Latency

**Problem**:
- Gradio tool calls have network latency
- Evidence static builds are fast but not real-time

**Solutions**:

1. **Caching Layer**:
   ```python
   from functools import lru_cache

   @lru_cache(maxsize=1000)
   def get_predictions(image_hash):
       return model(image)
   ```

2. **Progressive Loading**:
   - Load Evidence dashboard first (static, fast)
   - Lazy load Gradio components on demand

3. **WebSocket Connections**:
   - Use WebSocket for Gradio streaming
   - Reduce HTTP request overhead

---

### Challenge 4: Deployment Complexity

**Problem**: Managing multiple services (Gradio, Evidence build, MCP server).

**Solutions**:

1. **Docker Compose**:
   ```yaml
   version: '3.8'
   services:
     gradio:
       build: ./gradio-app
       ports:
         - "7860:7860"

     evidence:
       image: nginx:alpine
       volumes:
         - ./evidence-app/build:/usr/share/nginx/html
       ports:
         - "3000:80"

     mcp-server:
       build: ./mcp-orchestrator
       ports:
         - "8000:8000"
       environment:
         - GRADIO_URL=http://gradio:7860
         - EVIDENCE_URL=http://evidence:80
   ```

2. **Kubernetes**:
   - Deploy each service as a pod
   - Use ingress for routing
   - Service mesh for inter-service communication

3. **Serverless**:
   - Gradio on AWS Lambda (with container support)
   - Evidence on Netlify/Vercel
   - MCP server on Cloud Run

---

### Challenge 5: Version Compatibility

**Problem**: Updates to Gradio or Evidence may break MCP integration.

**Solutions**:

1. **Semantic Versioning**:
   ```python
   # Pin versions
   requirements.txt:
   gradio==4.8.0
   mcp-ui-server==0.2.1
   ```

2. **Integration Tests**:
   ```python
   def test_gradio_mcp_integration():
       # Test that Gradio MCP server responds correctly
       result = mcp_client.call_tool("classify", {"image": test_image})
       assert result.status == "success"
   ```

3. **Adapter Pattern**:
   ```python
   class GradioMCPAdapter:
       """Adapter to handle Gradio version differences"""
       def __init__(self, gradio_version):
           self.version = gradio_version

       def call_tool(self, name, args):
           if self.version.startswith("4."):
               return self._call_v4(name, args)
           else:
               return self._call_v5(name, args)
   ```

---

## Recommendations

### For Gradio + MCP-UI Integration

**✅ Recommended**:
- Use Gradio's native MCP server support (`mcp_server=True`)
- Expose ML models as MCP tools
- Use MCP resources for pre-built visualizations
- Keep Gradio UIs focused and single-purpose

**Best Practices**:
```python
# Good: Clear, focused Gradio app
demo = gr.Interface(
    fn=classify_image,
    inputs=gr.Image(),
    outputs=gr.Label(),
    mcp_server=True  # Enable MCP
)

# Also good: Multiple related tools
@demo.mcp_tool("classify")
def classify(image): ...

@demo.mcp_tool("explain")
def explain(image): ...
```

---

### For Evidence + MCP-UI Integration

**✅ Recommended**:
- Deploy Evidence as static site (Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages)
- Use MCP server to serve Evidence URLs as resources
- Pre-build Evidence dashboards for fast loading
- Use Evidence's templated pages for dynamic content

**Best Practices**:
```markdown
<!-- Evidence templated page: pages/model/[model_id].md -->
# Model: {params.model_id}

```sql model_stats
SELECT * FROM model_performance
WHERE model_id = '${params.model_id}'
```

<DataTable data={model_stats} />
```

Then MCP server can generate links:
```python
def get_model_dashboard(model_id):
    return url_resource(
        url=f"https://analytics.example.com/model/{model_id}",
        title=f"Model {model_id} Dashboard"
    )
```

---

### For Combined Architecture

**Architecture Recommendation**:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  Frontend (MCP-UI)                  │
│           React/Vue/Svelte + @mcp-ui/client         │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘
                     │ MCP Protocol (JSON-RPC)
┌────────────────────▼────────────────────────────────┐
│              MCP Orchestrator Server                │
│  - Authentication                                   │
│  - Resource routing                                 │
│  - Event coordination                               │
│  - Tool aggregation                                 │
└───────┬─────────────────────────┬───────────────────┘
        │                         │
        │                         │
┌───────▼──────────┐      ┌──────▼──────────┐
│  Gradio Service  │      │ Evidence Static │
│  (MCP Server)    │      │ (Hosted)        │
│                  │      │                 │
│  - ML Models     │      │  - Dashboards   │
│  - Real-time     │      │  - Analytics    │
│  - Interactive   │      │  - Reports      │
└──────────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
        │                         │
        └────────►[DB]◄───────────┘
              Shared Data Store
```

**Technology Stack**:

| Layer | Technology | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| **Frontend** | React + @mcp-ui/client | Render MCP-UI resources |
| **Orchestrator** | Python FastAPI + MCP SDK | Route requests, coordinate services |
| **ML Service** | Gradio + mcp_server=True | Interactive ML inference |
| **Analytics** | Evidence.dev (static build) | BI dashboards |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL / DuckDB | Shared data |
| **Cache** | Redis | Performance optimization |
| **Auth** | Auth0 / Keycloak | Unified authentication |

---

### Deployment Strategy

**Development**:
```bash
# Terminal 1: Gradio
cd gradio-app
python app.py  # Runs on :7860

# Terminal 2: Evidence
cd evidence-app
npm run dev  # Runs on :3000

# Terminal 3: MCP Server
cd mcp-server
python server.py  # Runs on :8000

# Terminal 4: Frontend
cd frontend
npm run dev  # Runs on :5173
```

**Production**:
```yaml
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'

services:
  mcp-server:
    build: ./mcp-server
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      - GRADIO_URL=http://gradio:7860
      - EVIDENCE_URL=https://analytics.example.com
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://db:5432/app
    depends_on:
      - db
      - gradio

  gradio:
    build: ./gradio-app
    ports:
      - "7860:7860"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://db:5432/app
    depends_on:
      - db

  frontend:
    build: ./frontend
    ports:
      - "80:80"
    environment:
      - MCP_SERVER_URL=http://mcp-server:8000

  db:
    image: postgres:15
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=app
      - POSTGRES_USER=user
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

---

## Conclusion

### Summary of Findings

**Can MCP-UI serve as an integration layer for Gradio and Evidence artifacts?**

**✅ YES** - MCP-UI can effectively integrate both:

1. **Gradio Integration**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (Excellent)
   - Native MCP server support
   - Bidirectional communication
   - Tools, resources, and prompts
   - Real-time interaction

2. **Evidence Integration**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (Very Good)
   - Static export to hosted URLs
   - Embed component support
   - Fast, scalable delivery
   - Limited to one-way communication

3. **Combined Architecture**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (Very Good)
   - Unified interface for ML + Analytics
   - Coordinated data flows
   - Single authentication
   - Rich user experience

### Key Advantages

1. **Gradio Strengths**:
   - Interactive ML demos
   - Real-time inference
   - Python-first development
   - Native MCP support

2. **Evidence Strengths**:
   - SQL-based analytics
   - Static export (fast, cheap)
   - Rich visualization library
   - Version control friendly

3. **MCP-UI Strengths**:
   - Protocol-based integration
   - Sandboxed security
   - Standardized interface
   - Multi-service orchestration

### Recommended Use Cases

| Use Case | Gradio | Evidence | MCP-UI |
|----------|--------|----------|--------|
| **ML Model Serving** | ✅ Primary | ❌ No | ✅ Orchestrator |
| **BI Dashboards** | ⚠️ Possible | ✅ Primary | ✅ Orchestrator |
| **Real-time Predictions** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | ✅ Coordinator |
| **Historical Analytics** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | ✅ Display |
| **Interactive Exploration** | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Limited | ✅ Yes |
| **Static Reports** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | ✅ Display |

### Future Opportunities

1. **Gradio ↔ Evidence Data Flow**:
   - Gradio predictions → Database → Evidence visualization
   - Real-time analytics pipeline

2. **MCP-UI Extensions**:
   - Custom renderers for Gradio/Evidence
   - Enhanced event system
   - State synchronization protocol

3. **Standardization**:
   - Common data formats
   - Shared component library
   - Unified theming

### Final Recommendation

**For maximum benefit, use all three together**:
- **Gradio** for interactive ML and data science workflows
- **Evidence** for polished, stakeholder-ready BI dashboards
- **MCP-UI** as the orchestration layer that brings them together

This combination provides:
- ✅ Best-in-class ML tooling (Gradio)
- ✅ Best-in-class BI tooling (Evidence)
- ✅ Standardized integration (MCP-UI)
- ✅ Unified user experience
- ✅ Production-ready architecture

---
